changes_summary: Addressed all 3 significant flags: (1) Added Step 5 covering internal clip drag path through `useClipDrag.ts` → `createTrackAndMoveClip()` → `addTrack()`, leveraging `addTrack`'s existing `index` parameter. (2) Added Step 3 fixing the broken plumbing — `isNewTrackTop` must be added to `DropIndicatorPosition` interface and forwarded in `toIndicatorPosition()`. (3) Added Step 4 changing the top indicator from normal-flow to absolute positioning to avoid displacing track rows and breaking coordinate math.
questions: The committed top indicator uses sky-blue dashed styling. Should it match the bottom drop zone exactly, or is a different treatment preferred?

Should the top drop zone activation threshold have a larger hot zone (e.g., 20px above the first row) to make it easier to trigger, or is the current threshold (cursor above row 0's top edge = rawRowIndex < 0) sufficient?
success_criteria: Effect-layer clips (2 tabs) show tabs stretched evenly across full panel width.
All other clip types (3-tab audio, 4-tab visual/text) render tabs correctly with even spacing.
Dragging any external item (file, text tool, effect layer, asset) above the first track shows a top drop indicator with correct label.
Dragging an existing clip above the first track shows the top drop indicator.
Dropping on the top indicator creates a new track at index 0 and shifts existing tracks down — for both external and internal drops.
The bottom drop zone continues to work identically (no regression).
The top indicator is absolutely positioned and does not shift track row layout during drag.
Hit-testing remains accurate: hovering over the first track row still targets that track, not the top drop zone.
assumptions: The committed top drop zone implementation (HEAD) is functionally correct for detection and insertion logic — only the indicator plumbing and positioning need fixes.
Track ordering is purely index-based (tracks[0] = topmost). Prepending to arrays or passing index=0 to addTrack() is sufficient.
The tab fix only needs TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS updates — no shared Tabs component changes needed.
addTrack() in editor-utils.ts:42-70 correctly handles index=0 insertion (splice at 0) for both tracks and rows arrays.
The absolute-positioned top indicator (pointer-events-none, z-10) won't interfere with drag event propagation to the underlying tracks.
createTrackAndMoveClip's downstream logic (finding the new track, moving the clip) works correctly regardless of where the track was inserted, since it references tracks by ID not index.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
